label: South Africa
countryDocumentSourceName: South African Department of Home Affairs
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Common source for passports and civil records.
typicalArrivalNotes: Long-haul movers often benefit from stronger pre-arrival planning.
Civil record replacement and legalisation review should not be left to the last minute.

label: India
countryDocumentSourceName: Ministry of External Affairs / local authorities
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Common source for passports, attestation and document services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document attestation and legalisation often take longer; plan early.
Education and civil records may need extra processing for use in the Netherlands.

label: United States
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Passports and apostille services vary by state.
typicalArrivalNotes: Apostille is often used for US documents; check state-level requirements.
Long-distance moves benefit from advance planning for shipping and first-week setup.

label: United Kingdom
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Official guidance on birth, marriage and legalisation services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Post-Brexit document validation may differ from EU routes; check current requirements.
Driving licence exchange rules for UK licence holders are set by RDW.

label: Canada
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and authentication services vary by province.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document authentication for use abroad often involves provincial and federal steps.
Long-haul logistics and temporary housing planning are often useful.

label: Australia
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Passports, birth certificates and apostille/authentication services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document legalisation for the Netherlands may involve DFAT authentication.
Long-distance moves often need extra buffer for shipping and first-month admin.

label: Germany
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from German authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU/EEA moves often have simpler document and driving licence pathways.
Registration and BSN sequencing still matter for a smooth start.

label: Switzerland
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Some Swiss documents can be used directly in the Netherlands; others need a Hague apostille—depends on document type and use.
typicalArrivalNotes: Swiss citizens follow IND guidance alongside EU/EEA nationals for many stay questions; focus on registration and BSN.
Driving and document rules are often closer to EU neighbours than to long-haul non-EU moves.

label: Sweden
countryDocumentSourceSummary: EU movers: many documents are straightforward; confirm unusual cases per Netherlands Worldwide legalisation overview.
typicalArrivalNotes: Swedish EU citizens focus on gemeente registration, BSN, insurance, and banking—not visa or MVV steps for ordinary moves.
Ferry, road, or short flights are common; plan first-week sequencing like other EU origins.

label: Denmark
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Some Danish documents immediate; diplomas/certificates often e-apostille; Danish may need sworn translation unless a multilingual form applies.
typicalArrivalNotes: Danish EU citizens prioritise registration within official timelines, BSN, insurance, and banking.
Road via Germany or short flights; document prep may include e-apostille for education credentials.

label: Norway
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Some Norwegian documents immediate; others need apostille from Norway—depends on document type and recipient.
typicalArrivalNotes: Norwegian EEA nationals follow IND EU/EEA/Swiss guidance for many stay questions; focus on registration, BSN, insurance, and banking—not MVV steps for ordinary moves.
Flights to Amsterdam are common; verify Norwegian document legalisation only when Netherlands Worldwide or the recipient requires it.

label: Ireland
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Some Irish documents immediate; others need apostille from Ireland—depends on document type and recipient.
typicalArrivalNotes: Irish EU citizens focus on gemeente registration, BSN, Dutch basic health insurance timing (per Government.nl), and banking—not visa or MVV steps for ordinary moves.
Short flights to Amsterdam or Eindhoven are common; confirm Irish document legalisation only when Netherlands Worldwide or the recipient requires it.

label: France
countryDocumentSourceName: Mairie / French civil and consular services
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from French authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU moves typically have lower document friction; focus on registration and first-week sequencing.
Driving licence use and exchange are covered by RDW and EU rules.

label: Spain
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from Spanish authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU/EEA document and mobility pathways usually apply.
Municipality registration and BSN remain the main early priorities.

label: Italy
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from Italian authorities (comune, anagrafe, or consular channels where relevant).
typicalArrivalNotes: EU/EEA document and mobility pathways usually apply.
Municipality registration and BSN remain the main early priorities.

label: United Arab Emirates
countryDocumentSourceName: UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs / issuing authorities
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Attestation for UAE-issued documents; passport-country documents may follow other legalisation routes.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV and residence permit sequencing is often top of mind after a Gulf-based application.
Keep attested documents and translations accessible for municipality or IND follow-up.

label: Brazil
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and document legalisation via cartórios and consulates.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document legalisation for use in the Netherlands can take time; start early.
Long-haul moves often benefit from shipping and temporary housing planning.

label: Mexico
countryDocumentSourceName: Registro Civil / Mexican competent authorities for apostille
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Mexican civil records and Hague apostille (Apostilla de La Haya) through the authority competent for each document.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV collection in Mexico and residence permit follow-up in the Netherlands often frame the first month.
Keep apostilled actas and translations accessible for gemeente or IND appointments.

label: Singapore
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Hague apostille for many Singapore-issued public documents; MFA describes SAL as competent authority.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV collection per your IND letter and residence permit follow-up often frame the first month.
Keep SAL-apostilled documents and translations accessible for gemeente or IND appointments.

label: Japan
countryDocumentSourceName: Japanese Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) — apostille
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Hague apostille for many Japan-issued public documents per Netherlands Worldwide guidance.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV steps per your IND letter and residence permit follow-up often frame the first month.
Keep MOFA-apostilled documents and translations accessible for gemeente or IND appointments.

label: South Korea
countryDocumentSourceName: South Korean authorities — apostille (per Netherlands Worldwide)
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Hague apostille for many South Korea–issued public documents; Korean documents often need translation for Dutch use.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV steps per your IND letter and residence permit follow-up often frame the first month.
Keep apostilled documents and accepted translations accessible for gemeente or IND appointments.

label: Türkiye
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Some Turkish documents can be used as-is in the Netherlands; others require apostille. Turkish-language documents often need translation into Dutch, English, French, or German for Dutch use.
typicalArrivalNotes: MVV and residence permit steps from Türkiye often frame the first month; follow your IND letter and Netherlands Worldwide appointment guidance.
Keep apostilled documents and accepted translations accessible for gemeente or IND follow-up.

label: Argentina
countryDocumentSourceName: Argentine Ministry of Foreign Affairs — digital e-apostille (per Netherlands Worldwide)
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Documents from Argentina need MFA digital e-apostille for Dutch use; translations may be required depending on document language.
typicalArrivalNotes: Long-haul arrival after MVV steps; Buenos Aires embassy context often frames pre-departure timing.
Keep e-apostilled documents and accepted translations accessible for gemeente or IND follow-up.

label: Chile
countryDocumentSourceName: Chilean authorities — Hague apostille (per Netherlands Worldwide)
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Documents from Chile need a Hague apostille from the Chilean authorities for Dutch use; translations may be required depending on document language.
typicalArrivalNotes: Long-haul arrival after MVV steps; Santiago embassy context often frames pre-departure timing.
Keep apostilled documents and accepted translations accessible for gemeente or IND follow-up.
